channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Enhancing Outcome Measurement in Oncology Clinical Trials Through Artificial Intelligence: A Scoping Review
item.description: This scoping review examines the application of artificial intelligence to enhance outcome measurement in oncology clinical trials. The review synthesizes evidence on how AI can improve the accuracy, efficiency, and standardization of outcome assessment across oncology trial designs and populations.

item.title: Application of plant viruses in biotechnology with relation to artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology
item.description: This review examines the application of plant viruses in biotechnology, with emphasis on their integration with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and nanotechnology approaches. The article explores how these emerging technologies can enhance the development and utilization of plant virus-ba

item.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
item.description: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.

item.title: Which ‘ai scientist’ suits your lab? a guide for the perplexed
item.description: General purpose AI tools for science, such as Claude Science, promise to accelerate research. But which is right for you?

item.title: Health system learning enables generalist neuroimaging models
item.description: NeuroVFM, trained on routine health system MRI and CT scans, learns general neuroimaging representations that improve diagnosis, report generation and triage, showing how private clinical data will power safer, more accurate medical AI.

item.title: Talk python to me: #554: trustworthy AI in healthcare and longevity
item.description: You ask an AI a question and it answers with total confidence. Most of the time, a confidently wrong answer is just an annoyance. But what if the question is medical, and there's a real patient on the other end? In that world, a hallucination isn't a bug, it'…

item.title: A call to action for an equitable global genomic system
item.description: As genomics infrastructure expands, current governance will determine whether it advances global health equity or entrenches disparities, locking biased data into future clinical tools. This Comment calls for inclusive, community-centered genomic systems, bef…

item.title: Openai says GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for microsoft copilot amid breakup chatter | techcrunch
item.description: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the 'preferred model' for Microsoft Copilot amid breakup chatter | TechCrunchtechcrunch.com

item.title: FDA Proposes Rule to Modernize Drug Manufacturing Registration
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a proposed rule that, if finalized, would create a streamlined registration pathway for distributed manufacturing establishments that operate as a single establishment using a “hub-and-spoke” model.

item.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts The AI firm Anthropic has got the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on ins

item.title: STAT+: The quest to save Grace — and clear the way for rare disease patients everywhere
item.description: How a father’s quest may shape the future of rare disease gene therapy.

item.title: Alchemab lands £25m from british business bank in record life sciences bet
item.description: Cambridge biotech Alchemab Therapeutics has extended its Series A round with a £25 million ($34 million) investment from the British Business Bank, which the company says is the state-owned lender’s largest backing of a life sciences company to date. The mone…

item.title: Biological aging clocks in health and disease
item.description: This review outlines the state of the art of biological clocks and how they could help understand pathobiology, reduce the burden of disease and extend healthspan.

item.title: Courts split over AI training fair use rulings [let's data science]
item.description: U.S. courts remain split on whether training AI models on copyrighted material is fair use, after **Judge William Alsup** ruled in June 2025 that training on purchased, digitized books was fair use while **Judge Vince Chhabria** reached the opposite conclusio…

item.title: Colorado’s admt comment window closes with no industry voice arguing for agent governance
item.description: Colorado’s recent pre-rulemaking comment period for the ADMT Act (SB 26-189) was defined by a conspicuous void. Between June 15 and July 13, 2026, stakeholders had a rare window to shape the regulatory future of automated decision-making. Yet, across the subm…

item.title: Human embryonic stem cell-derived dopaminergic cells for parkinson’s disease: a phase 1/2 open-label trial
item.description: A phase 1/2 trial of an off-the-shelf stem cell-derived dopaminergic progenitor therapy demonstrated a favorable 12-month safety profile, with no graft-related adverse events, dyskinesias or tumor formation, although risks were associated with the immunosuppr…

item.title: 85% of financial firms plan AI budget increases
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence (AI) is no longer just a technology project. It is becoming a line item with momentum. That’s the clearest signal from “The Enterprise AI Benchmark Report: Financial Services Pulls Ahead in the Enterprise AI Race,” a new PYM…

item.title: When the real world becomes the trial
item.description: Once confined to post-market surveillance, real-world evidence is now being used to emulate trials, guide approvals and even run studies in real time, forcing a rethink of what constitutes a clinical trial.

item.title: Anthropic's new "j-lens" reveals a silent workspace inside claude that mirrors a leading theory of consciousness | venturebeat
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude research reveals a hidden internal “global workspace” that resembles human conscious processing, raising major questions about AI reasoning, interpretability, safety, and machine consciousness.

item.title: Molecular design beyond training data with novel extended objective functionals of generative AI models driven by quantum annealing computer
item.description: This study presents a novel approach to molecular design that extends generative AI model capabilities beyond their training data through the use of extended objective functionals combined with quantum annealing computing. The method integrates quantum annealing technology to optimize molecular stru

item.title: Proteome-scale quantification of the interactions driving condensate formation of intrinsically disordered proteins
item.description: This study quantifies protein-protein interactions at the proteome scale that drive condensate formation by intrinsically disordered proteins, providing systematic characterization of the molecular mechanisms underlying phase separation. The research integrates experimental and computational approac

item.title: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) in neuropsychiatric drug discovery: current challenges and future directions
item.description: This review examines the application of artificial intelligence in neuropsychiatric drug discovery, identifying current technical and practical obstacles while proposing strategies to advance the field. The authors discuss how AI can address existing limitations in the drug development process for p

item.title: STAT+: 931 days. The drug approval scandal hiding in plain sight
item.description: Two years, six months, and 18 days have elapsed since Northwest Biotherapeutics submitted its brain cancer treatment for regulatory approval.

item.title: Fidji Simo steps down from OpenAI’s no. 2 role
item.description: OpenAI's No. 2 executive, Fidji Simo, is stepping down from her full-time role after her medical leave proved longer than expected — a leadership vacuum that comes at a tricky time as the company eyes a possible IPO and races to catch Anthropic in the enterprise market.

item.title: OpenAI launches its new family of models with GPT-5.6
item.description: OpenAI's latest family of models promises improvements across a range of areas, including cybersecurity.

item.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
item.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

item.title: New York Times says OpenAI hid evidence in ChatGPT copyright trial
item.description: News publishers say OpenAI hid tools and datasets that could identify copyrighted journalism in ChatGPT outputs, escalating their lawsuit with a new motion for sanctions.

item.title: How did the government decide OpenAI’s frontier model was safe to release?
item.description: "Exactly what that dialog looked like between the government and Anthropic and OpenAI is unclear."

item.title: Anthropic’s new Claude feature is quietly selling you on AI
item.description: Claude’s new Reflect dashboard doesn’t just visualize how you use AI. It also subtly reinforces how much of your daily work now depends on Anthropic’s chatbot.

item.title: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX are bigger than the last 25 years of tech exits
item.description: Three big AI IPOs are set to generate more value than all the U.S. VC-backed exits since 2000.

item.title: Popular open source AI developer tool Ollama raises $65M, grows to nearly 9M users
item.description: Benchmark-backed Ollama has amassed 176,000 stars, and nearly 17,000 forks on GitHub by helping developers easily run AI on their PCs.

item.title: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
item.description: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company built a tool called the

item.title: OpenAI may have made a fatal misstep in copyright fight with news orgs
item.description: OpenAI may be sanctioned for hiding, deleting ChatGPT logs in NYT copyright fight.

item.title: Mind the gap: an embedding guide to safely travel in sequence space
item.description: Author summary Enzymes are nature’s catalysts: proteins that accelerate essential chemical reactions. Engineers often want to redesign them, but even a single amino acid change can destroy function by disrupting the precisely shaped active site where catalysi…

item.title: Mindbeam sets generative AI models to task on drug design, hunting for better pain meds
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence infrastructure startup Mindbeam AI Inc. today published research showing how generative AI can aid in the discovery of safer pain-relief drugs. The company used acetaminophen, one of the most widely used over-the-counter pai…

item.title: Daily briefing: mutation lets octopuses make proteins with precision
item.description: A break in these octopuses’ protein-making machinery boosts their accuracy. Plus, a Nobel-prizewinner has left the United States for China and the effects of microgravity on the brain.

item.title: Amazon’s CTO on how developers can ride the ai-powered coding wave
item.description: Software engineering is going through its most dramatic transformation in years.

item.title: Bans on AI layoffs: current laws and what might come next
item.description: An appellate court in China ruled that employers cannot cite AI as a reason for terminating employees. Is similar legislation likely to occur in the U.S.?

item.title: Is recursive self‑improvement the dawning of AI superintelligence?
item.description: AI systems can now improve themselves by themselves. Is this the dawn of machine superintelligence?

item.title: The EU AI act deadline has moved, but data lineage can’t wait
item.description: Despite moving deadlines, data lineage remains crucial for AI initiatives.

item.title: Google hands open health stack to the linux foundation
item.description: BrianFagioli writes: The Linux Foundation intends to launch the Open Health Stack Software Foundation, a new vendor-neutral home for the Google Open Health Stack project. Google is contributing the project code and assets while Google.org is providing a $3 mi…

item.title: Dia and mhra launch new global regulatory science summit in london to advance safer and smarter health innovation
item.description: The DIA-MHRA Global Summit Unites Two World-Leading Organizations Around Regulatory Science, Scientific Innovation, Health Access, Patient Safety, and Cross-Sector Collaboration

item.title: The u.s. military just deployed AI that listens to doctor visits and writes the medical notes
item.description: The U.S. Military Health System is deploying AI that listens to clinical visits and auto-generates medical notes worldwide. Consent is required. Here's how it works.

item.title: Citi's AI robotics picks: rockwell, emerson, honeywell lead the list
item.description: Citi's Robotics & Physical AI Leadership Conference wrapped up on Tuesday. The annual Citi Research event brings together robotics founders, investors, operators, and industry executives to assess the state of "physical AI." Analyst Heath Terry summarized the…

item.title: Securing shadow it in the corporate environment
item.description: Every organization has two technology environments. The one that IT built, documented, and maintains visibility over, and the one that employees assembled around it to get their work done faster. The second environment is shadow IT, and in most enterprises, i…

item.title: What enterprises are getting wrong about AI data readiness
item.description: AI adoption is accelerating -- so are failures. Without proper data governance, quality controls and infrastructure, even the best AI tools will underperform or fail completely.

item.title: Secure it infrastructure: a practical guide for it leaders
item.description: This guide explains secure IT infrastructure, its core security pillars and how IT leaders can align investments with business goals, resilience and compliance.

item.title: Provision of preferred nutrients to macrophages promotes salmonella intracellular replication without relying on type iii secretion systems
item.description: Author summary Salmonella Typhimurium is a leading cause of foodborne illness globally and can cause systemic infections by surviving inside macrophages, the immune cells in charge of fighting them. Traditionally, it was believed that Salmonella relies strict…

item.title: Metabolic risk and metabolic dysfunction–associated steatotic liver disease and steatohepatitis in cognitive decline: a retrospective cohort study
item.description: Objective To determine whether metabolic risk factors (MRFs), metabolic dysfunction–associated steatotic liver disease (MASLD) and metabolic dysfunction–associated steatohepatitis (MASH) are associated with incident mild cognitive impairment (MCI), vascular d…

item.title: Determinants of intention to use generative AI fitness assistants: integrating the theory of planned behavior, second-order information system quality, and perceived value
item.description: As generative artificial intelligence (GAI) becomes embedded in sport and health management, GAI fitness assistants can generate and iteratively refine training plans through conversational interactions; however, their adoption remains shaped by users’ trade-…
